role: Iranian national charged in the UK under the National Security Act 2023 for surveillance of Iran International journalists on behalf of Iranian intelligence
bio:
Mostafa Sepahvand is an Iranian national who entered the United Kingdom in 2016 concealed in a lorry and claimed asylum. In May 2025 he was arrested and charged by the Metropolitan Police's Counter Terrorism Command alongside [Farhad Javadi Manesh](/people/farhad-javadi-manesh) and [Shapoor Qalehali Khani Noori](/people/shapoor-qalehali-khani-noori) under the National Security Act 2023 — the first Iranians charged under the legislation.

Sepahvand is accused of engaging in conduct likely to assist a foreign intelligence service, identified as Iran's [Ministry of Intelligence](/orgs/ministry-of-intelligence), between 14 August 2024 and 16 February 2025. He faces an additional charge under Section 18(1)(a) of the Act for conducting surveillance, reconnaissance, and open-source research with the intention of committing serious violence against a person in the United Kingdom. The targets were journalists at [Iran International](/orgs/iran-international), the London-based Farsi-language broadcaster that Iran has labelled a terrorist organisation.

All three defendants appeared at Westminster Magistrates' Court on 17 May 2025 and were remanded in custody. A plea hearing is scheduled for 26 September 2026 and trial for 5 October 2026 at Woolwich Crown Court.

sources.title: Three men charged with National Security Act offences
UK police charge three Iranians with suspected espionage
Three Iranians charged in UK spying case set to face trial

editorial_notes: Age 39 at time of charging (May 2025). Entered UK in 2016 in a lorry, claimed asylum. First Iranians charged under National Security Act 2023. Trial set for October 2026 at Woolwich Crown Court.
